pattern: Generate the target by applying the next-permutation step k times, then count the minimum adjacent swaps that turn the original digit string into that target by greedily bringing each target digit into place from left to right.
hints: The kth smallest larger permutation is found by repeating the standard next-greater-permutation transformation k times starting from the original string.
Once you have the target arrangement, the question reduces to the fewest adjacent transpositions to reorder one sequence into another with equal multiset of characters.
Walk both strings left to right; for each target position find the nearest matching unused character to the right in the working copy and bubble it left, adding the distance moved to the swap count.

edge_inputs.description: first wonderful permutation needs one swap
two-digit ascending number swaps once for the only larger permutation
leading zeros are kept as ordinary digits
repeated digits limit how many distinct larger permutations exist
a single trailing-suffix reversal can require several adjacent swaps
many equal digits mean far permutations are still close in swaps
larger k jumps further so the swap count grows

stress.description: random digit strings with small k from a tiny alphabet to force duplicates
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 60))
    num = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 3)) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 50)
    return [num, k]
stress.note: duplicates exercise the next-permutation tie handling and swap counting

stress.description: longer strings over all digits with moderate k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 120))
    num = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, 100)
    return [num, k]
stress.note: full digit range with sufficient length so k larger permutations exist
